channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Native kids with disabilities were held in wooden boxes. Sweeping reforms are coming
item.description: State officials in New York say the Salmon River district's special education program confined young children with disabilities in wooden boxes. Parents weren't notified.
item.content:encoded:
State officials in New York say the Salmon River district's special education program confined young children with disabilities in wooden boxes. Parents weren't notified.

(Image credit: Used with permision)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Linda McMahon defends dismantling the Education Department, shifting its work
item.description: The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.
item.content:encoded:
The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.

(Image credit: Eric Lee)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Jury orders Boeing to pay $49.5 million to family of 737 MAX crash victim
item.description: A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.
item.content:encoded:
A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.

(Image credit: Stumo Family)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: An LA-area mayor acted as an agent for China. Experts say it's part of a pattern
item.description: Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.
item.content:encoded:
Eileen Wang, now the former mayor of the City of Arcadia, agreed to plead guilty to one felony charge that she acted as an illegal foreign agent of China.

(Image credit: Frazer Harrison)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: The MAHA movement is coming to school cafeterias. Here's what that means for kids
item.description: U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. school districts worry it could get even more expensive to prepare a meal under new federal dietary guidelines, as they also contend with cuts to programs that helped them buy local food.

(Image credit: Rachel Wisniewski for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Trump's DEI blitz raises questions: Could the NFL's Rooney Rule be flagged next?
item.description: The Trump administration is challenging DEI policies at firms that seek diverse candidates through the hiring process. Could the NFL be next?
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration is challenging DEI policies at firms that seek diverse candidates through the hiring process. Could the NFL be next?
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu

item.title: Denise Powell wins Democratic primary for Nebraska's 2nd congressional district
item.description: Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.
item.content:encoded:
Political organizer Denise Powell has defeated State Sen. John Cavanaugh to win the Democratic primary in the closely watched race for Nebraska's second congressional district.

(Image credit: Rebecca S. Gratz)
item.dc:creator: Jason Breslow

item.title: Remains of 2nd U.S. soldier who went missing in Morocco have been recovered
item.description: Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.
item.content:encoded:
Spc. Mariyah Symone Collington, 19, of Taveres, Florida, is the second U.S. soldier who fell off a cliff during a recreational hike in Morocco. The remains of 1st Lt. Kendrick Lamont Key Jr. were recovered last week.

(Image credit: Mosa'ab Elshamy)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Foreign ticket holders from World Cup teams' countries won't have to pay bonds to enter U.S.
item.description: The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
The Trump administration is suspending a requirement that visitors from countries that have qualified for the World Cup and bought tickets for the tournament pay as much as $15,000 in bonds to enter the U.S.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Alex Murdaugh will get a new murder trial. Here's a timeline of his case
item.description: Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
Alex Murdaugh — the disgraced former lawyer serving a life term for the murders of his wife and son — will get a new trial in South Carolina, the state Supreme Court said on Wednesday.

(Image credit: Gavin McIntyre)
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell
